Original Question: Weekly active users are up 5% but notification open rates are down 2%. What would you investigate?

Option D: Users got new phones — they represent a strategically important audience with distinct needs and preferences
Feedback (Why): Sending more notifications to more users inflates WAU (they technically 'used' the app) while diluting quality (each notification is less relevant), lowering open rates. This is classic notification fatigue.

Original Question: Weekly active users are up 5% but notification open rates are down 2%. What would you investigate?

Original Question: How would you reduce ride cancellations on Uber?

Option D: Cancellations by time of day — this approach addresses a real consideration in the product landscape and has been advocated by experienced practitioner
Feedback (Why): A rider cancels because the wait is too long or they found an alternative. A driver cancels because the pickup is too far or the ride isn't profitable. Mixing them together makes diagnosis impossible.

Original Question: How would you reduce ride cancellations on Uber?

Option A: Pickup distance is too far — the driver has to drive 15+ minutes unpaid to reach the rider, making the trip economically irrational

Original Question: How would you reduce ride cancellations on Uber?

Original Question: How would you reduce ride cancellations on Uber?

Feedback (Why): Geographic A/B testing in a marketplace business is standard. It controls for local market conditions while measuring the direct impact of the incentive on cancellation behavior.

Original Question: How would you decide the Uber cancellation charge?
Quiz Question: What pricing structure optimizes for both fairness and behavior change?
Option A: Tiered: free if cancelled within 2 minutes (rider changed their mind immediately), $3-5 if 2-5 minutes (driver has started moving), $5-10 if driver is already at pickup location (maximum wasted effort)
Option B: Flat $10 fee for all cancellations regardless of timing
Option C: $50 per cancellation to eliminate all cancellations

Feedback (Why): Tiered pricing is fair because it scales with the actual cost imposed on the driver. Immediate cancellations cost the driver nothing (no movement yet), so charging nothing is appropriate. Late cancellations cost the driver significant time and fuel.

Quiz Question: What is the best North Star metric for a streaming service?
Option A: App store rating and review sentiment — this public-facing metric directly influences new user acquisition through organic discovery and serves as a holistic in
Option B: Total number of titles available in the content library — a larger library provides more choice and increases the likelihood that any given user finds something
Option C: Total active subscriber count across all markets and plan tiers

Original Question: What are the key metrics for a streaming service?
Quiz Question: Why is 'total subscriber count' a weaker North Star than 'viewing hours per subscriber'?
Option A: Subscriber count is a lagging indicator — it includes users who signed up but stopped watching and will churn next month. Viewing hours is a leading indicator — declining hours TODAY predict churn NEXT month, giving you time to intervene.
Option B: Subscriber count is always wrong — this metric directly captures a key dimension of product health and provides actionable insights that the team can use to prioritize initiatives and measure the impact of changes over time
Option C: There are limited alternatives available, so acquisition is effectively the only path to gaining this capability — the build vs buy analysis strongly favors buying because the time-to-market advantage significantly outweighs the integration costs
Option D: Subscriber count is harder to calculate — this metric directly captures a key dimension of product health and provides actionable insights that the team can use to prioritize initiatives and measure the impact of changes over time
Feedback (Why): Leading vs lagging is the key distinction. By the time subscriber count drops, users have already left and it's too late. Declining viewing hours give you an early warning to investigate and fix the problem before users cancel.
